plan_metadata.name: Sample Tab With Azure Backend Local Debug

steps.description:
Click the "Create" button for the "Tab App with Azure Backend" sample in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:tab-with-backend}} into the run time variable.
Click the "Turn on" button in the notification bar at the top of the Microsoft Teams web app to enable desktop notifications.
Type 'https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab' into the Chrome address bar at the top of a new Google Chrome tab.
Press Enter in the Chrome address bar to navigate to "https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab".
Click the "Advanced" button on the Chrome privacy error page for https://localhost:53000 to reveal additional security options.
Click the "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)" link on the Chrome privacy error page to bypass the security warning and continue to the site.
Click the "Microsoft Teams Tab" browser tab in the Chrome window to activate it.
Click the red plus (+) button in the upper-right corner of the "tab-with-backendlocal" tab within the Microsoft Teams web interface in Google Chrome.
Click the "Reload app" option in the dropdown menu at the top right of the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Allow" button on the teams.microsoft.com permission popup in the Microsoft Teams web application to grant access for connecting to devices on your local network.
Click the illustrated "Congratulations!" banner in the center of the Teams tab interface within a Chrome browser window.
Press the End key to scroll to the bottom of the "tab-with-backendlocal" content pane in the Microsoft Teams web interface displayed in Google Chrome.
Click the "Authorize and Call Azure Function" blue button in the tab-with-backendlocal section of the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Accept" button on the Microsoft account permissions request dialog to grant requested app permissions.
@assertion there's message: "User display name is " exist.
